title: Use start_async for blocking work in handle_event
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/export_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.ExportLive). handle_event("export", _params, socket) must not block — it should call start_async(:export, fn -> MyApp.Exporter.run_heavy_export() end) and immediately return. A handle_async(:export, {:ok, result}, socket) clause assigns the result as :export_result and sets :exporting? false. mount/3 returns {:ok, assign(socket, export_result: nil, exporting?: false)}.
tier_rationale: start_async/3 + handle_async/3 is a modern pattern that LLMs miss, often using Task.async instead.
research_citation: Hex Shift top 10 mistakes: 'Blocking work on the LiveView process'
